Gold.data_anomaly: Gold price $3992.40 is outside expected range ($4000-$7000). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: The S&P 500 slipped 0.5% on Thursday, with the Nasdaq dropping 1.6% as a sharp rotation out of semiconductors and AI names gathered force. TSMC reported a 77% profit jump and raised its full-year capex guide to $60-64B, but the stock sold off 3.2% as investors fretted about free cash flow compression in the AI buildout. ASML's quarter beat and guidance raise could not arrest the slide—the semis selloff that started with TSMC spilled across the memory complex, hitting SK Hynix, Micron, and Samsung hard. Separately, Alphabet fell 4.7% after a reported delay to its Gemini 3.5 Pro AI model. Oil held near $79 as the US-Iran naval standoff and Hormuz disruption continued, though profit taking after the 14% surge capped gains. Gold broke below $4,000, with a data anomaly raising questions about the exact print. The macro backdrop saw cooling June CPI (3.7% YoY) and a still-hawkish Fed Chair Warsh, leaving rate-hike odds finely balanced ahead of the July 29 FOMC decision.
commodities_note: WTI slipped 0.1% to $79.17 as profit taking emerged after the 10.9% five-day surge on Hormuz tensions. Gold is posted at $3,992—outside the expected $4,000-7,000 range; the pipeline flags a data anomaly, likely from a contract rollover or unit mismatch, so treat this spot gold figure as suspect until reconfirmed. The US10Y yield drifted up to 4.57%, and the VIX rose 6.8% to 16.7 as equity volatility ticked up.

thesis_highlights: ASML High-NA EUV / Intel 14A ramp in H2 2026 is the structural catalyst. Entry before that milestone.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to WATCH — ASML back within 4% of its 50-day, risk/reward improved to 2.4:1.
writeup.what_to_do: The 1.4% drop on Thursday brought ASML to $1,784.87, within the 2-5% neutral zone above the SMA50. The company's record Q2 (€9.3B revenue, raised full-year guide to €43-45B per ASML IR, Jul 15) confirms insatiable High-NA EUV demand for AI chip production. R:R is now 2.4:1 to resistance at $1,959, though the wide-stop R:R using the $1,441 structural support is only 0.5:1—so entry must be sized carefully. Consider a starter entry now with a tight mental stop below the 50-day, or wait for a more definitive pullback toward $1,720.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if High-NA EUV customer ramp (Intel 14A) (H2 2026) is delayed, cancelled, or underwhelms market expectations.

writeup.headline: Memory selloff deepens — 5d -14%, three consecutive beats now discounted.
writeup.what_to_do: MU dropped another 4.8% to $853.20, now 8.2% below its still-rising SMA50. The FQ3 numbers were stellar (revenue $41.5B, EPS $25.11), but the forward PE of 5.7x shows the market is pricing a memory peak, not a super-cycle. With HBM4 fully booked through 2027 and a $200B capex plan, the structural conviction remains high, but the technical damage is severe. Watch for a higher low above the $652 support zone—the next real floor—to signal the selloff is exhausted. No entry until then.
writeup.valuation_judgment: 77% discount to the semis cluster median PE — the cheapest name in the cluster, but the memory cycle peak fear is overriding valuation support.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if MU fails to reclaim SMA50 ($929.40) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

thesis_highlights: TSMC CoWoS packaging sold out through end-2026 — capacity is the bottleneck, not demand.
Cramer commentary on TSMC ('more business than they can handle') is directionally consistent with CoWoS thesis.

writeup.headline: TSMC sold off on capex raise despite record profit — R:R not actionable.
writeup.what_to_do: The 3.2% decline post-earnings brings TSM to $409.74, below its 50-day average. The Q2 beat was stellar (77% profit jump, revenue $40.2B), but the capex raise to $60-64B raised FCF fears. The headline R:R is 2.1:1, but the invalidation at $404.56 is only 1.3% away, and the nearest resistance at $413.53 is just 0.9% above—the trade range is completely compressed. Reassess once the stock clears $430 (old support) with conviction; until then, this is dead money.
writeup.valuation_judgment: Trades at a 23% discount to the semis cluster median PE — reasonable if the capex-induced FCF crunch eases.
writeup.tight_upside_disclosure: Nearest resistance at $413.53 (0.9% above) — headline R:R only materialises once that level breaks on above-average volume. Any upgrade to WATCH or BUY is conditional on $413.53 breaking on above-average volume.

Generic fundamental praise does NOT clear the bar — default to WATCH if neither anchor applies.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to WATCH — deeply oversold, 2.2:1 R:R, earnings risk 10 days out.
writeup.what_to_do: NVTS cratered 11.4% to $11.76, now 43% below its SMA50 and with RSI 31.5—approaching capitulation territory. The R:R to $17.79 is 2.2:1, with a wide-stop ratio of 1.84:1 passing the structural test. However, the July 27 earnings binary (pre-earnings band: +13.2%/-18.6%) overshadows any technical signal. Size only a very small starter if you believe the data-center GaN pivot materializes; otherwise, wait for the print. Relative strength: lagging AI Power Infrastructure by 23.7% over the month.

writeup.headline: PLTR holds $134 but 64x PE remains the choke point.

The DoD Maven program-of-record arc is a strong narrative, but the 64x forward PE demands 30%+ revenue growth for years. With no immediate catalyst and the R:R distorted by a tight stop, there's no reason to chase. Maintain CAUTION until the stock clears $140 on volume, showing momentum behind the defense thesis.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster faces a classic defensive rotation—UOB's pullback narrows its extension, but DBS and OCBC remain overbought. NIM compression is a structural overhang with no relief until at least October's MAS review, while the strongest revenue growth is coming from wealth management, not lending. The cluster's direction hinges on the US10Y breaking sustainably below 4.20%, which hasn't happened.
singapore.key_development: DBS targets >$774B wealth AUM by 2030, a bullish structural goal but one that does not alter short-term NIM dynamics.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster experienced a severe drawdown, with SOXX plunging 4.2% on the day and 8.8% for the week. TSMC's capex-fueled FCF fears infected the entire supply chain, hitting memory stocks the hardest: MU, SK Hynix, and Samsung all fell 5-11%. The AI capex debate is now firmly in 'show me the ROI' territory, and the market is demanding proof that spending translates to profits. Only ASML's strong guidance offered any counter-narrative, but it wasn't enough to lift the group.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally intact but tactically broken — capex digestion is the new risk
semiconductors.key_development: TSMC raised capex to $60-64B (from $52-56B), initially positive for long-term capacity, but the immediate FCF dent overwhelmed investor sentiment.

big_tech.summary: BigTech was dragged lower by GOOG's 4.7% drop on the Gemini AI delay, which overshadowed positive developments like MSFT's revamped OpenAI deal. The cluster's aggregate PE is now near the low end of its historical range, but earnings risk is concentrated in the next two weeks: GOOG reports July 22, MSFT and AMZN on July 29-30. The capex sustainability question will be the make-or-break theme for Q2 prints.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but AI spending credibility is being tested
big_tech.key_development: GOOG's Gemini 3.5 Pro delay raises doubts about its AI execution, while Amazon's $25.5B bond sale signals continued mega-capex.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's other $2B photonics partner, an S&P 500 member with InP capacity doubling by end-CY2026 — direct beneficiary of the optoelectronics buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels and the next hyperscaler capex print (GOOG Jul 22) confirms no AI spending rollback.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and data-center switching plays that directly tie to AVGO's and NVDA's networking expansion.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if MRVL falls below its 200-day moving average and the AI networking theme survives the post-earnings consolidation.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking upgrades benefit Arista — the NOK AI networking thesis shares a read-through on hyperscaler switch spend.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after Nokia's July 23 earnings for a clearer read on AI networking demand; a 10% pullback in Arista from current levels would be the entry zone.

active_risks: US-Iran naval blockade: Strait of Hormuz transit fee proposed, shipping disrupted; oil flows from Gulf at risk, Brent above $85.
Tariff war expansion: US imposes 25% tariffs on Brazil under Section 301; blanket tariff investigations targeting 60 countries could escalate protectionism.
Ukraine drone strikes: 136 vessels hit in the Sea of Azov, Russia threatening retaliation; energy and grain supply risks persist.
China economic drag: Q2 GDP growing at slowest pace in over three years; weak consumption undermines global demand recovery.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil above $79 keeps a supply-side inflation fear in play, pressuring Fed policy and tech multiples. The semis selloff was notably sharper than the broader market, suggesting that AI-spending fatigue is taking a firm hold. Singapore banks are moving on macro rate expectations; the memory complex is discounting demand weakness as the capex cycle peaks.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Brazil condemned the 25% US tariffs; the US trade representative cited a range of unfair practices. ASML posted a record Q2 beat and raised guidance, but markets ignored the positive in favor of the TSMC capex overhang. The CDC/IMF flagged higher global shipping insurance rates as Hormuz disruption intensifies.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, oil in the $75-85 range; semis digest the capex surge but no new AI demand shock; Q2 earnings broadly meet but fail to re-rate the sector. Defensive rotation favours value over growth.

optimistic.description: A ceasefire or diplomatic off-ramp emerges in the Hormuz, oil drops to ~$65; inflation fears fade, Fed signals a pause, easing financial conditions. Semis rally on renewed AI optimism from hyperscaler Q2 calls.

pessimistic.description: Iran blocks the Strait of Bab el-Mandeb, raising the prospect of dual-chokepoint disruption; oil spikes above $100, forcing a Fed rate hike. Semis and BigTech sell off further on recession fears, Singapore banks hit on margin compression.

wildcard.description: A US-China trade deal or broad tariff rollback, or a breakthrough in Ukrainian negotiations, reshapes global supply chains overnight, causing a violent sector rotation into beaten-down industrials.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Deploy no fresh capital until the semis selloff shows signs of exhaustion and oil volatility subsides. Hold existing positions; the single-factor AI-capex concentration is uncomfortably high given the brewing digestion concerns.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — semis drawdown deepens, energy disrupts rate outlook
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 3 of 31 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; the semis cluster's 4-10% daily declines are the dominant risk, with supercycle momentum abruptly reversed.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
